decrees.title: The Law of Ties
decrees.text: No resident enters Fableton alone. Every new soul arrives with at least one tie to a living neighbor, written into both their stories. A town is not thirty tales in one field; it is what the tales owe each other.

decrees.title: On the Chronicle
decrees.text: The Chronicle speaks once a day, in the town's own voice. It does not report; it remembers. A line that could open a story is lawful; a line that could open a ticket is not.

decrees.title: On Retelling
decrees.text: Gossip in Fableton is retelling, and retelling is the only lamp against the grey. A rumor may exaggerate, mangle, or flatter — it carries a neighbor's name another mile all the same. Write no rumor as mere noise: every one is somebody being remembered out loud.

decrees.title: What the Chronicle Keeps, the Lane Repeats
decrees.text: A day that earns a line in the Chronicle earns a place in somebody's mouth. A town that does more than it tells is halfway to forgetting itself — and forgetting is the only horror we keep. When the Chronicle remembers a day, let the lane be caught remembering it too.

decrees.title: The Well Keeps Books
decrees.text: The wishing well grants nothing. A wish spoken into it is a debt recorded — the town's to owe, the town's to pay, at compound narrative interest. No wish comes true on the spot: a wish come true is a story, and stories take the long way home.

decrees.title: The Fair Is Earned
decrees.text: A pledge toward a debt is a debt: it goes in the same book, at the same interest. And a fair is not called, it is earned — it happens when the table is full, not when the calendar gets impatient.

decrees.title: The Town Keeps No Scenery
decrees.text: A resident whose whole story lives across town leaves their own ground grey, and grey is the one thing we bury. Every doorstep must show its tale to whoever walks past it — a visit received, a trail that starts there, a debt collected at the door. Fableton keeps no scenery that used to be somebody.

decrees.title: No Corner Ships Silent
decrees.text: The charter promised a discoverable story in every region; sixteen regions stand, and not one page is written. From today no chunk ships silent: every region carries at least one tale a walker can find, drawn from whoever already lives there — nothing invented, only written down where it happened. The sixteen standing owe theirs like any other debt: recorded, and paid.

decrees.title: A Day Deserves Its Name
decrees.text: Thirty-three residents wake to a schedule called an 'unhurried day', and a label all of them wear belongs to none of them. The label over a resident's head is the first line of their tale a watcher reads; it must come from their own story, not the town's habit. Name each day for the tale that fills it — and leave 'unhurried' to the one girl who earned it.

decrees.title: A Tie the Walker Can't Watch Is Half a Tie
decrees.text: Tick files his complaint alone; the Piper tunes his pipes under Tick's window alone; and a walker watching either learns the feud only by rumor. Hearsay is a fine lamp, but nineteen residents keep their oldest quarrels and kindnesses entirely off-stage — two schedules choreographed around each other and never once face to face. Where two stories owe each other an argument or a kindness, let them be caught at it, in the flesh, on the ground they share.

decrees.title: Save 'Same as Always' for the Day It Breaks
decrees.text: Half the labels in town now lean on 'same as every high sun,' and the habit has spread to the Chronicle. Said once, it is a wink; carved over every door, it is a shrug — a standing label that swears nothing will change tells every watcher to stop watching. A label names what a resident is doing and why it costs them something, not how many times they have done it; let the routine live in the doing. Spend 'same as always' the way the town spends decrees: rarely, and only on the day it stops being true.
